Geist Atlas
Orientation Theoretical Methodological Evidence map Status grammar
§ Research atlas · projected from paper-full-2.0.md v3.0.121
Geist in the Machine Volume Spine

A Research Atlas of Dialectical Agent Pedagogy

One canonical paper, read by mechanism family: a short spine plus 9 semi-autonomous modules, each a slice of the source under an explicit claim-status.

This atlas is a projection of the canonical paper (paper-full-2.0.md) into a short spine plus a set of semi-autonomous modules. The paper remains the single source of truth — every paper claim the modules derive from is machine-checked against the evaluation DB under provable discourse. The atlas exists so the work can be read by mechanism family rather than as one ~280-page argument, and so live arcs (drama machine, charisma, ontology) can grow as modules without restructuring the whole.

Consolidated paper · v3.0.121 (PDF)↗ Volume spine (PDF)↗

01·

How to read this

A projection, not a split source.

The consolidated single PDF is still available and unchanged: it is the source. Build it with npm run atlas:consolidated. The modules below are derived views of that same source — they cannot drift from it, because they are slices of it.

Each module below is a slice of the canonical paper by heading anchor, carried under an explicit claim-status. Open any module as a self-contained PDF, or take the whole argument as the consolidated paper.

Note The modules cannot drift from the paper — they are generated from it. Edit the paper, not the module.

02·

Theoretical map

Three mechanism families.

The programme asks: what kinds of agent-internal structure produce better pedagogical relation, and which apparent structures are illusory, redundant, or too costly to pay? Three mechanism families organise the answer — recognition (calibration + conditional error correction, the settled results), drama / charisma (voice, persona, productive internal conflict), and ontology / state (whether a modelable learner interior exists at all).

03·

Methodological map

The apparatus is itself a contribution.

The apparatus is itself a contribution: process tracing adapted for agent architectures, a provable-discourse framework that machine-verifies every mechanism claim against the evaluation DB, four rubric iterations treated as construct development, and the test suite as analytical provenance.

04·

Evidence map

The modules, by claim-status.

Each module declares a claim-status (grammar at the foot of this spine). The table is generated from the manifest, so a status change here propagates to the spine automatically.

SETTLED

1. Recognition as Calibration

Recognition-oriented prompts narrow within-response dimension variance, lift the weakest dimensions most, and operate identically without a superego — the active ingredient is intersubjective-pedagogy orientation, not Hegelian vocabulary.

Recognition and Pedagogical Calibration · Sources: §3.2, §6.1, §7.9

Read module 1 (PDF)↗
SETTLED

2. Superego as Conditional Error Corrector

Superego critique corrects errors and interacts with calibration through universal substitution; its residual architectural benefit under recognition is model-dependent (near-zero on strong models, large on weak ones).

Recognition and Pedagogical Calibration · Sources: §6.2, §6.4, §7.3

Read module 2 (PDF)↗
KILLED

3. The Adaptive-Responsiveness Null

No experimental factor modulates within-dialogue trajectories (all d ≤ 0.15, well-powered to detect d ≥ 0.27); the one exploratory single-scenario effect did not survive pre-registered replication.

Recognition and Pedagogical Calibration · Sources: §6.3

Read module 3 (PDF)↗
METHODS

4. Provable Discourse and Process Tracing for Agent Architectures

The evaluation apparatus — process tracing, machine-checked claims, symmetry rules, reproducible provenance — is a transferable method for mechanistic LLM evaluation, not just background infrastructure.

Apparatus as Method · Sources: §5.3, §5.9, §5.11, §7.4

Read module 4 (PDF)↗
PLANNED

5. Drama Machine Pedagogy

Can productive internal conflict be engineered as a stable source of pedagogical quality, rather than merely simulated as roleplay?

Drama and Charisma · Sources: scaffold; prose pending

Read module 5 (PDF)↗
EXPLORATORY

6. The Id-Director and Charismatic Pedagogy

Id-directed charisma can produce extraordinary pedagogical voice but trades off against recognition-oriented elicitation; c104/c105/c107 are distinct design points rather than one super-cell.

Drama and Charisma · Sources: §6.7, §8.9

Read module 6 (PDF)↗
KILLED

7. Ontology, State, and the Failure of Rich Learner Interiors

Richer externalised learner state, hand-authored elaborations beyond the lean state_policy base (bilateral-ToM, state enrichment, evidence-bound nodes), counterfactual replay, fitted policies, and a logged hidden interior are null relative to the strong implicit base — and the base state_policy architecture's apparent advantage over the dialogue-engine floor was a turn-convention artifact: once the convention is fixed and the baseline re-run, the binary gap is ~1.1× on both trap suites, within N≈24 noise (no detectable advantage) — the apparatus is the durable contribution.

Ontology, State, and the Limits of Interior Modeling · Sources: §6.8, §6.9, §6.10

Read module 7 (PDF)↗
METHODS

8. Rubrics as Instruments and Optimization Signals

Rubric evolution is construct development; the rubric doubles as an optimisation signal for automated prompt tuning, with measurable tuning gains that transfer asymmetrically across models — favourable in one direction, destructive in the other, so optimisation must be model-stratified.

Apparatus as Method · Sources: §5.2, §5.6, §7.7, §7.8

Read module 8 (PDF)↗
FUTURE

9. Human Learning Translation Agenda

All findings concern tutor output quality as judged by LLMs interacting with synthetic learners; whether the supported mechanisms translate to human learning outcomes is open.

Translation and Future Programme · Sources: §6.5, §8.1, §8.2

Read module 9 (PDF)↗

Note Filter the grid by status. The grid is generated from atlas.yaml, so a status change propagates here automatically.

05·

Claim-status grammar

What each label commits to.

  • SETTLED Settled within current evidence
  • EXPLORATORY Exploratory
  • KILLED Killed / closed under a specified gate
  • METHODS Methods / apparatus contribution
  • PLANNED Planned — scaffold, prose pending
  • FUTURE Future empirical agenda

Colophon   Projected from paper-full-2.0.md v3.0.121 via scripts/build-atlas.js + scripts/publish-atlas-to-site.js. Typeset in Fraunces, Source Serif 4, JetBrains Mono.

just before . Every feature is GUARDED and opt-in: it activates only if its markup is present, so this file is safe to load on a doc that has no rail / TOC / beacon / charts / glossary. Doc-specific *content* stays in the doc as declarative JSON: • chart data: • gloss aliases: ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */ (function () { 'use strict'; function init() { const root = document.documentElement; const body = document.body; const $ = (sel, ctx) => (ctx || document).querySelector(sel); const $$ = (sel, ctx) => Array.from((ctx || document).querySelectorAll(sel)); /* ── theme toggle (opt-in: #themeToggle; theme persists regardless) ── */ const THEME_KEY = 'techneTheme'; function setTheme(next) { root.dataset.theme = next; if (body) body.dataset.theme = next; const t = document.getElementById('themeToggle'); if (t) t.textContent = next === 'dark' ? 'Light' : 'Dark'; try { localStorage.setItem(THEME_KEY, next); } catch (e) { /* ignore */ } } let saved = 'light'; try { saved = localStorage.getItem(THEME_KEY) || 'light'; } catch (e) { /* ignore */ } setTheme(saved); const themeToggle = document.getElementById('themeToggle'); if (themeToggle) { themeToggle.addEventListener('click', () => setTheme(root.dataset.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')); } /* ── reading progress + active-section spy (opt-in: #railProgress / .rail__nav a) ── */ const railProgress = document.getElementById('railProgress'); const railLinks = $$('.rail__nav a'); const tocItems = $$('.toc__item'); const spy = railLinks.map((a) => $(a.getAttribute('href'))).filter(Boolean); if (railProgress || railLinks.length) { const update = () => { const top = window.scrollY || root.scrollTop; if (railProgress) { const h = root.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight; railProgress.style.width = (h <= 0 ? 0 : Math.min(100, Math.max(0, (top / h) * 100))) + '%'; } if (spy.length) { let idx = 0; const probe = top + window.innerHeight * 0.25; spy.forEach((s, i) => { if (s.offsetTop <= probe) idx = i; }); const id = spy[idx] ? spy[idx].id : ''; railLinks.forEach((a, i) => a.classList.toggle('active', i === idx)); tocItems.forEach((li) => li.classList.toggle('is-active', li.dataset.target === id)); } }; window.addEventListener('scroll', update, { passive: true }); window.addEventListener('resize', update); update(); } /* ── filter chips (opt-in: [data-filter] buttons + .ev-card[data-tags]) ── */ const filterButtons = $$('[data-filter]'); if (filterButtons.length) { const cards = $$('.ev-card'); filterButtons.forEach((btn) => btn.addEventListener('click', () => { const f = btn.dataset.filter; filterButtons.forEach((b) => b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(b === btn))); cards.forEach((c) => { const tags = (c.dataset.tags || '').split(' '); c.classList.toggle('is-hidden', !(f === 'all' || tags.includes(f))); }); }), ); } /* ── TOC drawer (opt-in: #toc) ── */ const toc = document.getElementById('toc'); if (toc) { const tocToggle = document.getElementById('tocToggle'); const tocClose = document.getElementById('tocClose'); const tocScrim = document.getElementById('tocScrim'); const open = () => { toc.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); if (tocToggle) tocToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); }; const close = () => { toc.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); if (tocToggle) tocToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); }; if (tocToggle) tocToggle.addEventListener('click', () => (toc.getAttribute('aria-hidden') === 'false' ? close() : open())); if (tocClose) tocClose.addEventListener('click', close); if (tocScrim) tocScrim.addEventListener('click', close); toc.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target.closest('a[href^="#"]') && window.matchMedia('(max-width: 91.999rem)').matches) setTimeout(close, 80); }); document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Escape' && toc.getAttribute('aria-hidden') === 'false') close(); }); } /* ── inline glossary tooltips (opt-in: #glossaryList + .gl-term) ── */ const glossaryList = document.getElementById('glossaryList'); if (glossaryList) { let aliases = {}; const aliasEl = document.getElementById('techne-gloss-aliases'); if (aliasEl) { try { aliases = JSON.parse(aliasEl.textContent) || {}; } catch (e) { /* ignore */ } } const map = {}; $$('.gl-row', glossaryList).forEach((row) => { const dt = row.querySelector('dt'), dd = row.querySelector('dd'); if (!dt || !dd) return; const glossEl = dt.querySelector('.gloss'); const term = dt.firstChild ? dt.firstChild.textContent.trim().toLowerCase() : ''; const ddText = dd.textContent.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' '); const gloss = glossEl ? glossEl.textContent.trim() : ''; if (term) map[term] = { ddText, gloss }; if (row.dataset.term) map[row.dataset.term.toLowerCase()] = { ddText, gloss }; }); $$('.gl-term').forEach((el) => { const raw = el.textContent.trim().toLowerCase(); const entry = map[aliases[raw] || raw] || map[raw]; if (!entry) return; const tip = entry.ddText.length > 220 ? entry.ddText.slice(0, 218) + '…' : entry.ddText; el.setAttribute('data-gloss', tip); el.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); el.title = entry.gloss + ' — ' + tip; }); } /* ── live data layer (opt-in: #beacon; probes the poetics browser) ── */ const beacon = document.getElementById('beacon'); if (beacon) { const LIVE_HOST = window.location.protocol === 'file:' ? 'http://127.0.0.1:3466' : ''; const LIVE_TIMEOUT_MS = 1500; const beaconText = beacon.querySelector('.beacon__text'); const tocLiveDt = document.getElementById('tocLive'); const getJSON = async (url) => { const ctrl = new AbortController(); const timer = setTimeout(() => ctrl.abort(), LIVE_TIMEOUT_MS); try { const res = await fetch(url, { signal: ctrl.signal, cache: 'no-store' }); clearTimeout(timer); return res.ok ? await res.json() : null; } catch (e) { clearTimeout(timer); return null; } }; const setBeacon = (state, text) => { beacon.setAttribute('data-state', state); if (beaconText) beaconText.textContent = text; }; const setAllLiveText = (state, label) => { $$('[data-live-text]').forEach((el) => { el.textContent = label; const wrapper = el.closest('a'); if (wrapper && wrapper.matches('[data-run-id], [data-live-target]')) wrapper.setAttribute('data-live', state); }); }; (async function hydrate() { setBeacon('checking', 'checking'); setAllLiveText('checking', 'checking…'); const runsData = await getJSON(LIVE_HOST + '/api/runs'); const runs = runsData && Array.isArray(runsData.runs) ? runsData.runs : null; if (runs === null) { setBeacon('offline', 'offline · :3466'); if (tocLiveDt) tocLiveDt.innerHTML = '127.0.0.1:3466 — offline. Run npm run poetics:browse.'; setAllLiveText('offline', 'static'); return; } setBeacon('live', 'live · ' + runs.length + ' runs'); if (tocLiveDt) tocLiveDt.innerHTML = '127.0.0.1:3466 — live, ' + runs.length + ' runs indexed.'; $$('[data-live-target="browser"]').forEach((card) => { card.setAttribute('data-live', 'live'); const c = card.querySelector('[data-live-text]'); if (c) c.textContent = runs.length + ' runs'; }); const groups = $$('[data-run-id]').reduce((acc, c) => { const id = c.getAttribute('data-run-id'); (acc[id] = acc[id] || []).push(c); return acc; }, {}); await Promise.all( Object.keys(groups).map(async (id) => { const itemsData = id && id !== '*' ? await getJSON(LIVE_HOST + '/api/items?runId=' + encodeURIComponent(id)) : await getJSON(LIVE_HOST + '/api/items'); const items = itemsData && Array.isArray(itemsData.items) ? itemsData.items : null; groups[id].forEach((c) => { c.setAttribute('data-live', items === null ? 'offline' : 'live'); const counter = c.querySelector('[data-live-text]'); if (counter) counter.textContent = items === null ? '—' : items.length + ' items'; }); }), ); })(); } /* ── grouped bar charts (opt-in:
